summary_oneline: Evolve the existing IntentRouterMixin to add a deterministic e5-multilingual embedding router (LLM only as a discrepancy tie-breaker), wired into both ask() and conversation()
synthesis_summary: The brainstorm proposes a NEW IntentRouterMixin for deterministic embedding-based output-mode routing, but research shows an IntentRouterMixin already exists (parrot/bots/mixins/intent_router.py) doing pre-RAG retrieval-strategy routing over conversation() via a keyword fast-path plus an LLM invoke() decision. Rather than create a colliding class, the work (per user direction) is to FIX/evolve the existing mixin: add an e5-multilingual SentenceTransformer embedding router as the primary deterministic decision path, demote the LLM call to a tie-breaker used only when the embedding decision is ambiguous (discrepancy), and extend the hook to cover ask() in addition to conversation(). The output-mode contract (OutputMode enum + response.output_mode + ask(output_mode=) kwarg) already exists, so the router fills output_mode only when the caller left it unset. The encoder stack (sentence-transformers) is already vendored, so no new runtime dependency is required.

localization.role: Existing mixin to evolve — currently keyword+LLM retrieval-strategy routing over conversation()
Current single hook point; must be generalized and also reached from ask()
Keyword fast path to be supplemented/replaced by embedding routing
LLM decision path to be demoted to discrepancy tie-breaker
Config to extend with embedding model name, per-route threshold, route utterances
Agent base the mixin composes in front of
Distinct REQUEST entrypoints both accepting ctx: RequestContext; hook target for both
Existing output-mode contract the router sets (MAP/TABLE/CHART/INFOGRAPHIC/STRUCTURED_*)
Named per-request carrier (contextvar-bound); lacks output_mode/intent_score fields today
Subclass that overrides ask() with its own output_mode kwarg, bypassing conversation()
Optional encoder abstraction to reuse; sentence-transformers already vendored

constraints.text: Name + concept collision: IntentRouterMixin already exists and routes RETRIEVAL strategy, not OUTPUT mode. Per user direction the existing class is evolved (not duplicated), so retrieval-strategy behavior must be preserved and the new output-mode routing added alongside it.
constraints.implication: Backwards compatibility for current conversation()-based retrieval routing is mandatory.

constraints.text: The output_mode contract already exists on the AIMessage/response and as an ask() kwarg, not on RequestContext.
constraints.implication: Router fills output_mode only when caller left it None; precedence explicit-kwarg > router > default. Adding ctx.output_mode/intent_score is optional plumbing, not the canonical store.

constraints.text: ask() and conversation() are distinct; subclasses (DataAgent) override ask() independently and do not funnel through conversation().
constraints.implication: Routing wired only into conversation() misses ask(); a shared private hook must be invoked from both.

constraints.text: sentence-transformers is already a present runtime dependency, loaded lazily elsewhere.
constraints.implication: e5-multilingual adds zero new hard dependency; follow the lazy-import + load-once-in-configure pattern. Encode() must run off the event loop.

constraints.text: abstract.py (ask/conversation) and the mixin are both under active churn.
constraints.implication: Keep the base-class change to a narrow named hook (template-method A3) to minimize merge risk; avoid a full ask() override.

recent_history.author: Jesus Lara
recent_history.message: feat: TASK-1091 — IntentRouterMixin branch logic for ContextEnvelope

whats_new: IntentRouter embedding engine (e5-multilingual via sentence-transformers): encode route utterances once, max-cosine score per route, abstain below per-route threshold
A discrepancy/tie-break policy: invoke the existing LLM route ONLY when the embedding decision is ambiguous (top-2 routes within a margin, or below threshold but non-trivial)
A shared private hook (e.g. _resolve_output_mode / _route_intent) invoked from BOTH ask() and conversation()
IntentRouterConfig extension: embedding_model (default intfloat/multilingual-e5-small), per-route threshold, discrepancy margin, route utterances for output modes
whats_changed: parrot/bots/mixins/intent_router.py::IntentRouterMixin — add embedding router + demote LLM to tie-breaker; generalize hook to ask()+conversation() (F006, F010)
parrot/bots/abstract.py — add a minimal named no-op hook called from ask() and conversation() (F010, F003)
(optional) parrot/utils/helpers.py::RequestContext — add output_mode/intent_score fields if the contract should also live on the carrier (F004)
whats_untouched: OutputMode enum (already complete) — only consumed, not changed (F007)
Downstream output-mode rendering in data.py/visualizations — unchanged (F007)
patterns_to_follow: Lazy `import sentence_transformers as _st` + load-once in configure() (F005, F002)
Cooperative super().__init__/configure() chaining already used by the mixin (F006, F001)
asyncio.to_thread for blocking encode() to keep the event loop free (F005)
integration_risks: Reusing the name IntentRouterMixin for two routing concerns (retrieval + output mode) risks conceptual overload — consider clearly separated config sections / methods, or a sibling OutputModeRouter composed inside the mixin (F006)
Touching abstract.ask()/conversation() during active visualization churn — keep the hook tiny (F003)
e5 requires query/passage prefixing; wrong prefixing silently degrades similarity and invalidates tuned thresholds (F005)

confidence_map.claim: An IntentRouterMixin already exists and routes retrieval strategy (not output mode) over conversation()
confidence_map.reasoning: Full file read of the implementation

confidence_map.claim: The existing mixin uses keyword fast-path + LLM invoke() — the approach the brainstorm wanted to avoid
OutputMode + response.output_mode + ask(output_mode=) already form the output-mode contract
confidence_map.reasoning: Enum source + multiple assignment sites read

confidence_map.claim: RequestContext exists (contextvar-bound) but lacks output_mode/intent_score fields
ask() and conversation() are distinct; ask() is overridden independently by subclasses and not funneled through conversation()
sentence-transformers is already vendored; e5 adds no new hard dependency
A prior intent-router spec already exists and the subsystem is actively maintained
confidence_map.reasoning: Files on disk + recent git history

confidence_map.claim: RenderPhase exists but is scoped to prompt-layer caching, not general lifecycle dispatch
confidence_map.reasoning: Enum read; usage inferred from layer files only

confidence_map.claim: Best design is to evolve the existing mixin (embedding primary, LLM tie-breaker, hook ask()+conversation())
confidence_map.reasoning: Directly confirmed by user direction; consistent with findings

unknowns.question: Should output-mode routing live as a second concern inside the existing IntentRouterMixin, or as a separate OutputModeRouter engine composed by the mixin?
unknowns.user_answer: Fix/evolve the existing IntentRouterMixin to use the e5 embedding model; keep the LLM path only for resolving discrepancies; involve both ask() and conversation().

unknowns.question: What exactly constitutes a 'discrepancy' that should trigger the LLM tie-breaker — below threshold, or top-2 routes within a margin?
Should output_mode/intent_score also be added to RequestContext, or remain only on the response + ask() kwarg as today?

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence and the work is a bounded evolution of an existing, specced subsystem with the architecture already chosen by the user (e5 embedding primary + LLM discrepancy fallback + ask()/conversation() hook). Two minor unknowns (discrepancy definition, ctx field) are spec-time decisions, not blockers.
